key_insight: Surface reconstructions of GaN(0001)-(6×6) can be exhaustively enumerated as a tiling problem, and the tiling decomposition multiplicity n_til acts as a strong predictor of stability.
engineering_relevance: This turns an intractable first-principles search over hundreds of thousands of candidate surfaces into a much smaller, symmetry-organized set for DFT ranking, improving reliability and efficiency in surface-structure design.
method: Enumerated all rhombus tilings of the GaN(0001)-(6×6) surface lattice under the electron counting rule.
Built all compatible adatom configurations at fixed stoichiometry: 3 Ga adatoms and 18 H atoms, giving 416,683 total structures.
Used a machine-learning interatomic potential for screening and DFT for final validation/ranking of 24 candidates.
evidence_signal: The rule was tested across 14 Ga placement classes, reproduced by independent DFT calculations, and validated using a machine-learning potential trained/checked against 710 DFT-computed structures.
watch_item: The tiling rule is not purely sufficient by itself: a compatible tiling must exist, and that condition can add its own energy cost even when n_til is high.
paper_title: Tiling decomposition multiplicity predicts stability of GaN(0001) surface reconstructions
